title: salix purpurea subsp. helix
intro: Salix purpurea subsp. helix, also known as the curly willow or black willow, is a subspecies of the willow tree (Salix purpurea) that is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix purpurea subsp. helix include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as aiding in the treatment of fever, headaches, and menstrual cramps. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health issues.
